added: Risk Assessment replaced with flat risk table — auto-detected risks from data, curated migration defaults, and user-added rows with traffic light status, inline-editable mitigation plans, and category filtering
DOCX report: hypervisor-dependent tooling incompatibility warnings in Migration Readiness risks and new Section 4.1.6 with VMware-to-IBM Cloud tool replacement mapping table
Dynamic cost factor in Platform Selection questionnaire — 'Is cost an important factor?' automatically favours whichever platform has lower estimated monthly cost
All ROKS and All VSI cost tiles moved into Platform Selection tab for at-a-glance cost context
VM Assignment table defaults to platform selection questionnaire's favoured platform — SAP/Oracle VMs default to PowerVS, neutral falls back to auto-classification rules
VM Assignment table UI overhaul — workload type column, target dropdown, editable reason text, per-row reset button
Target IBM Cloud MZR dropdown on Infrastructure tab — auto-selects nearest MZR from source data center, user-overridable
Bulk Add Notes batch action in Discovery VM table — set the same note on multiple selected VMs at once
Multi-select status filter in Discovery VM table — combine filters like Unclassified + Included to find VMs needing classification
Unclassified option in Discovery status filter — quickly filter to VMs without a workload classification
DOCX report: callout in Platform Selection section when questions are answered 'Not Sure' or left unanswered
DOCX report: Coexistence Network Considerations subsection in Migration Strategy warning about WAN latency during migration, plus coexistence bullets in ROKS and VSI sections
Platform Selection questionnaire: 'No Preference' answer option for deliberately neutral responses — 'Not Sure' now means unanswered and is excluded from the answered count
Migration Planning tab in Migration Review — replaces Migration Timeline with integrated wave planning, AI wave analysis, and Gantt timeline driven by actual wave count

ROV (Red Hat OpenShift Virtualization) auto-derived from platform selection — when ROKS leaning with no container needs, suggests ROV at reduced OCP licence cost with All ROV cost tile and recommendation callout
removed: Cost Comparison tab removed from Migration Comparison — cost tiles now live in the Platform Selection tab
Wave Planning tabs removed from ROKS and VSI migration pages — wave planning is now consolidated in the Migration Planning tab of Migration Review
changed: Migration Timeline wave count now derived from wave planning active waves instead of port group estimation
fixed: Cost Estimation region now defaults to the MZR selected in Discovery's Infrastructure tab instead of always defaulting to us-south

added: ODF tier selection (Advanced/Essentials) dropdown in ROKS cost estimation panel — Essentials tier is ~20% cheaper
Optional ACM (Red Hat Advanced Cluster Management) per-vCPU pricing toggle in ROKS cost estimation with estimated rates
fixed: Removed stale VITE_IBM_CLOUD_API_KEY reference from README (was never used in app code)

added: Data Quality Warnings in Sizing Calculator — rule-based detection of suspicious VM resource allocations (memory/CPU outliers, ratio anomalies, name-resource mismatches) without requiring AI
Per-VM Node Fit Check in Sizing Calculator — validates every individual VM (with virtualization overhead) fits within selected bare metal node capacity, flags oversized VMs with remediation guidance

Platform Selection Questionnaire on Migration Review page — 12-factor decision matrix (5 VSI, 7 ROKS) to capture organizational and strategic platform preferences, with score summary and recommendation banner integration
AI Infrastructure: Tiered model selection — lightweight model (Granite 3.1 8B) for fast tasks, larger model (Granite 3.1 34B) for complex reasoning
AI Infrastructure: Server-Sent Events (SSE) streaming for chat and insights — text appears progressively instead of waiting for full response
AI Infrastructure: Persistent chat history — conversation preserved across navigation via localStorage with environment fingerprint scoping
AI Infrastructure: Parallel batch processing with concurrency limit (5 concurrent LLM calls) for faster classification and right-sizing of large environments
AI Feature: Target Selection — AI-powered ROKS vs VPC VSI classification with confidence scores and reasoning for each VM
AI Feature: Wave Sequencing — dependency detection, risk-aware scheduling, and VM move recommendations for migration wave planning
AI Feature: Anomaly Detection — client-side statistical analysis (z-scores, outlier detection) combined with AI narrative and remediation recommendations
AI Feature: Risk Analysis — AI-enhanced risk assessment with severity adjustment suggestions, missed risk detection, and security risk recommendations
AI Feature: Report Narrative — AI-generated executive summaries, environment analysis, migration recommendations, and cost justifications for exports
AI Feature: Discovery Questions — structured interview questions organized by topic (Business, Technical, Risk, Operations, Dependencies) with priority ranking
AI Feature: Interactive Interview — step-by-step consultant-style interview with insights extraction and persistent answer history
AI Chat: Markdown rendering (bold, code blocks, lists), Stop generating button during streaming, enriched context with network topology, OS distribution, and resource consumer data
New components: AIAnomalyPanel, AIRiskPanel, AIReportPreview, DiscoveryQuestionsPanel, InterviewPanel
changed: Target Classification: Replaced hardcoded 7-rule heuristic with data-driven rule engine (targetClassificationRules.json) — Windows VMs now classified by OS compatibility data instead of blanket Windows→VSI rule
AI proxy upgraded from single model to tiered model selection (fast vs complex tasks)
Chat panel now uses streaming by default for better responsiveness
Chat context builder now includes enriched data slices (network, OS, snapshots, datastores) for more accurate AI responses
Classification and right-sizing batch processing upgraded from sequential to parallel with configurable concurrency

fixed: Stale chunk loading after deployment — app now auto-reloads when navigating to a route whose code bundle changed
nginx now returns 404 for missing hashed assets instead of serving index.html as JavaScript
Added proper cache headers: hashed assets cached immutably, index.html never cached

added: Migration Review page — side-by-side ROKS vs VSI analysis with auto-classification, cost comparison, readiness metrics, architecture fit, and migration effort tabs
VM target classification service with 7-rule heuristic engine (OS compatibility, memory, workload type, Linux default)
VM-to-target assignment overrides with localStorage persistence and environment fingerprinting
Recommendation banner with data-driven migration strategy suggestion (All ROKS, All VSI, or Split)
Downloadable diagnostic log bundle on Settings page — exports anonymized app logs, environment info, and state snapshot for remote troubleshooting

fixed: VSI cost calculation no longer silently drops VMs with unrecognized profiles — uses closest same-family profile as fallback with estimated pricing note
Proxy pricing data now merges with static profiles instead of replacing entirely, preventing profile loss when proxy returns fewer profiles

changed: Moved Risk Assessment from Assess menu to Migration Assessment (after Pre-Flight Report) and merged two tabs into a single flat view
Infrastructure Details moved to appear between Dashboard and Discovery in the side navigation for earlier visibility
Moved Network Design from standalone Assess page to a tab within VSI Migration page for a streamlined workflow
ODF resource reservations now use per-component calculations based on Red Hat ODF tuning profiles (lean/balanced/performance) instead of simplified base+perDevice formula
Cluster-wide ODF components (MON, MGR, MDS) are distributed across actual node count, reducing per-node overhead on larger clusters
Overhead Reference page ODF section now shows profile-selectable per-component resource tables
added: ODF Tuning Profile selector (lean/balanced/performance) in ROKS Sizing Calculator with per-component CPU and memory breakdown
RADOS Gateway (RGW/S3) toggle — off by default since VM workloads typically don't need object storage
ODF CPU unit mode toggle — switch between physical cores and vCPU display for ODF reservations
Warning banner when lean ODF profile is selected (not recommended for OCP Virt live migration)
Enhanced ODF breakdown bar tooltips showing per-component resource allocation

changed: Replaced ~30 hardcoded hex colors with Carbon Design System CSS custom properties (var(--cds-*)) for dark mode compatibility across 9 component and page files
Replaced native HTML button elements in NetworkSummaryTable save/cancel with Carbon Button components using Checkmark/Close icons
Standardized tooltip trigger buttons with shared .tooltip-trigger CSS class instead of inline style={{ all: 'unset' }} across StorageTierSelector, WorkloadVMTable, and VSISizingPanel
Added aria-hidden="true" to decorative icons in NextStepBanner and EnhancedDataTable for improved accessibility
added: Support for vInfo-only RVTools files — only the vInfo sheet is now required; all other sheets are optional with graceful degradation
Empty state pages for Storage, Network, Hosts, and Clusters when their required sheets are missing
Navigation items are greyed out when data for their pages is unavailable (Storage, Network, Clusters, Hosts, Network Design)
InlineNotification on Network Design page when vNetwork sheet is missing
ROKS cost estimation now includes OpenShift Container Platform (OCP) license fees per vCPU-hour ($0.04275/vCPU-hr) across all worker nodes
ROKS cost estimation now includes OpenShift Data Foundation (ODF) Advanced storage costs — per-node for converged (NVMe) and per-vCPU for hybrid (VSI) architectures
Pricing update script fetches OCP license and ODF rates from IBM Cloud Global Catalog (containers-kubernetes service)
New Assess step in navigation between Prepare and Migrate with 3 pages: Risk Assessment, Migration Timeline, and Network Design
Red-Flag Risk Assessment page with 5 domains (Cost, Infrastructure, Complexity, Security, Other) — auto-calculated from pre-flight checks and complexity scores
Go/No-Go decision banner based on risk severity levels across all domains
Pre-Assessment Summary tab with environment snapshot, risk heat map, and key blockers list
Risk domain severity overrides with notes — persisted to localStorage
Migration Timeline page with Gantt chart visualization and configurable phase durations
Timeline estimation service with preparation, pilot, production waves, validation, and buffer phases
VPC Network Design page — maps VMware port groups to IBM Cloud VPC subnets across 3 availability zones
VPC topology D3 diagram with zone/subnet visualization
Auto-generated security groups from workload classification with template-based rules
ACL suggestion generation per subnet
Transit Gateway configuration (VPC, Classic, Direct Link connection types)
DOCX export integration for Risk Assessment, Timeline, and Network Design sections
Editable subnet table (name, CIDR, zone) and security group rule display

fixed: Pricing update script now resolves all 173 VSI and 26 bare metal profile prices (previously only 41 VSI and 11 bare metal had non-zero pricing)
Updated ibmCloudConfig.json with current IBM Cloud pricing for all profiles
changed: Rewrote pricing update script to navigate IBM Cloud Global Catalog plan hierarchy instead of relying on limited bulk search
Pricing script now supports three pricing models: gen2 component-based (per-vCPU + per-GB), gen3+ flat per-profile, and bare metal flat per-server
Added Z-series (LinuxONE) pricing support via Secure Execution vCPU/memory rates
Enhanced pricing update summary with per-profile missing pricing report

added: vInventory-to-RVTools converter script (scripts/convert_vinventory.py) — converts vInventory Excel exports to RVTools-compatible format for app ingestion
Synthesized vTools, vCPU, vMemory sheets from vInventory vmInfo columns
Synthesized vDatastore sheet from vDisk, DatastoreAssociation, and LUN data (capacity, usage, host mappings)
npm run convert:vinventory command for vInventory file conversion
CI/CD pipeline with GitHub Actions for lint, test, build, and E2E testing
CORS origin restrictions on all 3 Code Engine proxies (configurable via ALLOWED_ORIGINS env var)
Rate limiting (60 req/min) on pricing-proxy and profiles-proxy
Security headers in nginx.conf (X-Frame-Options, X-Content-Type-Options, Referrer-Policy, CSP)
Skip-to-content accessibility link in top navigation
Chart and table accessibility attributes (role, aria-label, title, description)
E2E tests for file upload, migration pages, and discovery workflow
Guided workflow stepper showing Upload → Review → Prepare → Migrate → Export progress
Next Step banners on Dashboard, Discovery, ROKS Migration, and VSI Migration pages
Export & Reports page consolidating all export options into a visible workflow step
Network page cross-link to Discovery Networks tab for subnet mapping
Guided Tour modal on Landing page with 5-step walkthrough of the migration workflow
"Take a Tour" button on Landing page for first-time user onboarding
Brief/detailed toggle in tour steps for progressive disclosure
"Restart Tour" option on Settings page to replay the guided tour
SectionErrorBoundary component for resilient per-section error handling with retry
changed: Replaced verbose console.log calls in hooks/services with structured logger (debug-only in production)
SideNav restructured from 21 flat items to 8 top-level entries with collapsible groups (Infrastructure Details, Migration Assessment, Reference)
Discovery promoted to second position in navigation after Dashboard
AI Assistant removed from SideNav (accessible via floating ChatWidget)
Workflow stepper is now always visible when data is loaded (dismiss button removed)
Major component refactoring: DashboardPage, SizingCalculator, DiscoveryVMTable, VSIMigrationPage, and TablesPage decomposed into smaller, focused components
Extracted business logic into custom hooks (useDashboardData, useSizingCalculator, useVSIPageData, useDiscoveryVMTableActions)
Cost estimation service refactored with improved validation and structure
Deployment docs updated to reflect CORS/ALLOWED_ORIGINS security model (removed stale AI_PROXY_API_KEY references)
security: Removed client-side API key (VITE_AI_PROXY_API_KEY) that was bundled into JavaScript — key was exposed in browser source
Removed AI proxy client authentication middleware (authenticateApiKey) — CORS restrictions now handle access control

added: AI wave planning suggestions with per-wave risk narratives and dependency warnings (POST /api/wave-suggestions)
AI cost optimization recommendations with prioritized savings estimates for VSI (POST /api/cost-optimization)
AI remediation guidance with step-by-step instructions for migration blockers (POST /api/remediation)
AI Wave Analysis panel in Wave Planning tab on ROKS and VSI migration pages
AI Cost Optimization panel in Cost Estimation tab on VSI migration page
AI Remediation Guidance panel in Pre-Flight Checks tab on ROKS and VSI migration pages
AI-enhanced export reports:
— DOCX reports include AI executive summary, risk assessment, strategy, recommendations, and cost optimizations
— PDF reports include dedicated AI Migration Insights page
— Excel reports include AI Recommendations sheet
— BOM Excel reports include AI Notes sheet with cost optimization guidance
— All AI content includes watsonx.ai disclaimer and graceful degradation when AI is unavailable
25 future ROKS bare metal profiles (bx3d, cx3d, mx3d, mx3de, ux3de, mx2de, bx3, cx3, mx3) added as custom profiles in ibmCloudConfig.json
changed: Sizing Calculator now defaults to the best-value (cheapest) ROKS+NVMe bare metal profile instead of the first in the list
Clicking a bare metal profile tile in ROKS Cost Estimation switches to the Sizing tab with that profile pre-selected
ROKS Cost Estimation profile comparison tiles now display custom profile tags (e.g., 'Future', 'On-Prem') as purple badges
Custom bare metal profiles with no pricing data now show 'Unable to Price' instead of '$0' in cost estimation summary, line items, and profile comparison tiles
Renamed 'Workload Discovery' to 'Discovery' in navigation and page title
Discovery page now uses tabs: 'Workload' (existing content) and 'Networks' (Network Summary table)
Moved Network Summary table from Network page to Discovery > Networks tab
Extracted NetworkSummaryTable into reusable component (src/components/network/)

added: AI-powered features using IBM watsonx.ai via Code Engine proxy:
— Workload classification with confidence scoring and reasoning (purple 'AI' tags)
— VSI right-sizing recommendations with cost optimization insights
— Migration insights panel with executive summary, risk assessment, and recommendations
— Context-aware chatbot for migration planning (floating widget and full-page at /chat)
— Settings page (/settings) with AI toggle, proxy connectivity test, consent, and cache management
— AI status indicators across the app (SideNav, chat widget, insights panel)
Unified single-page Workload Discovery layout replacing previous multi-tab design:
— Metric cards: Total VMs, Classified VMs, Unclassified, and VMs in Scope
— Interactive chart linked to VM table — click a bar to filter by workload type
— Workload type info tile showing description and detection patterns for selected type
— Unified VM table showing ALL VMs with Workload Type, Status, and inline actions
— Workload type filter bar with Unclassified tile for VMs needing categorization
Four-tier classification precedence: User > Maintainer > AI > Rule-based pattern match
— User overrides (via UI) take highest priority
— Maintainer authoritative classifications (authoritativeClassifications in workloadPatterns.json) — AI cannot override, shown with teal 'Maintainer' tag
— AI classification fills gaps when available
— Rule-based pattern matching as fallback when no AI
Fully maintainer-configurable auto-exclusion rules in workloadPatterns.json (no hardcoded logic):
— fieldRules for VM properties (template, powerState)
— namePatterns for VM name matching (contains, startsWith, endsWith, exact, regex with excludePatterns)
— Templates, powered-off, VMware infrastructure, AD/DNS, and network edge appliances auto-excluded
— Users can force-include or manually exclude via UI
VM management features: exclude/include VMs, bulk operations, workload type overrides, per-VM notes, CSV export, JSON export/import of overrides
Custom bare metal profiles for ROKS Sizing Calculator — define future ROKS hardware in ibmCloudConfig.json
Migration-relevant descriptions for all 14 workload types in workloadPatterns.json
Maintainer guide (_maintainerGuide) in workloadPatterns.json explaining all three config sections
changed: Discovery page redesigned from multi-tab layout to unified single page with chart-to-table linking
Classification and auto-exclusion are independent systems — a VM can have a workload type AND be auto-excluded
All auto-exclusion logic moved from hardcoded TypeScript to JSON-driven rules in workloadPatterns.json
Standardized user-facing terminology to 'Workload Type' across all UI and documentation
Migration pages (VSI, ROKS) use unified exclusion logic: force-included > manually excluded > auto-excluded > default included
fixed: cust-edge and service-edge VMs correctly identified as network appliances (not falsely excluded as VMware infrastructure)
ROKS Sizing Calculator excludes user-excluded VMs from CPU, memory, and storage calculations
VSI cost estimation storage total only includes disks from non-excluded powered-on VMs

added: Readiness Score info tooltip on ROKS and VSI migration pages explaining score calculation and thresholds
changed: ROKS Sizing Calculator: Renamed 'Headroom' to 'Ceph Reserve' with clearer description of its relationship to Operational Capacity
ROKS Sizing Calculator: Total requirement cards now show combined workload + infrastructure requirements with breakdown details
About page footer now links to actual GitHub repository
fixed: Consistent vertical spacing between UI elements across all pages by adding margin to Carbon Grid columns
About page sections (Application Info, Technology Stack, Changelog, Resources) now have proper spacing between rows
ROKS Sizing Calculator: Storage overhead renamed from 'Virt. Overhead' to 'Storage Overhead' for clarity

added: Comprehensive User Guide page with step-by-step instructions for all features

Edit icon and visual styling for editable subnet cells with hover effects
Instruction banner above Network Summary table explaining inline editing
Toggle control on Network page to show all VM labels in network topology diagram
VMs with Multiple NICs table now filters by migration scope (excluded VMs hidden)
Wave Planning panel displays all waves (removed previous 8-10 wave limit)
WaveVMTable component with clickable wave filters and VM details table
Clusters page VM table showing VMs filtered by selected cluster
Storage page VM table showing VMs filtered by selected datastore (derived from vDisk paths)
FilterableVMTable reusable component for entity-based VM filtering
CPU virtualization overhead calculated automatically: 0.27 vCPU per VM (fixed) + 3% of guest vCPUs (proportional)
Memory virtualization overhead calculated automatically: 378 MiB per VM (fixed) + 3% of guest RAM (proportional)
CPU, Memory, and Storage cluster capacity visualizations with 5 segments: VM requirements, Virt overhead, ODF reserved, System reserved, Free capacity
Breakdown summary text below each visualization showing Total, Used, Free, and Utilization percentage
ResourceBreakdownBar reusable component for visualizing resource allocation with color-coded segments
Virtualization overhead configuration file (src/data/virtualizationOverhead.json) with detailed per-component values
Overhead Reference page (/overhead-reference) displaying actual overhead values from JSON with calculation formulas
Ceph Reserve display below Operational Capacity slider showing linked percentage value
changed: Updated page title from 'temp-app' to 'VCF Migration Planning'

Wave Planning 'Overview' renamed to 'All' and now displays all VMs in table

ROKS Sizing Calculator: Renamed 'OpenShift Virtualization Overhead' slider to 'Storage Virtualization Overhead' for clarity

ROKS Sizing Calculator: Renamed requirement cards to 'VM vCPU Requirements', 'VM Memory Requirements', 'VM Storage Requirements' for clarity
